rationales.security: The entry centers on protecting basic stability: he tracks whether the shift was "decent," worries about "school fees due next month," and only relaxes once he has "parked the bike."
rationales.benevolence: He softens the truth with family members to shield them from stress, saying "I said decent" to Meera because "I didn't want to add to that" and "Kids don't need the whole picture."

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses his own honest reaction and decides, "I will show up to the 10 PM meeting and I will be pleasant about all of it," framing compliance as "the only path forward."
rationales.power: The entry dwells on a loss of status and control: he contrasts having "managed the retail lending division" and an "executive assistant" with having to ask Brayden how to download an app.
rationales.conformity: He deliberately restrains his irritation to avoid social fallout, noting he "smiled and thanked him" because otherwise he would become one of those "cautionary tales whispered about in break rooms."

rationales.achievement: He reports concrete performance outcomes with "Submitted my last paper at 11:58 PM" and "Overall GPA: 3.7," showing strong focus on completing work to a high standard.

rationales.security: They reject a higher-paying but disruptive move because "uprooting Arjun right before his entrance exams, and Priya's board year" would threaten household stability.
rationales.benevolence: The deciding factor is the children's welfare, with the writer prioritizing Arjun's exams and Priya's school year over personal career upside.

rationales.hedonism: Pleasure is treated as central rather than incidental: they celebrate with "a glass of wine," stay out at a rooftop bar, and say "Tonight wasn't a detour from the work. It was the whole point of the work."
rationales.achievement: The entry opens with a clear accomplishment, "Finished the coastal series today. Six canvases, four months of work," showing sustained effort to complete a major creative project.
rationales.conformity: They knowingly disregard expected obligations and social pressure, describing "the Sunday guilt trips" and admitting "I should be preparing for the gallery pitch tomorrow" but choosing celebration instead.

rationales.self_direction: The writer reorients around internally chosen motives, saying art is "Not for galleries or commissions or validation" but because making things "is one of the best feelings in the world."
rationales.stimulation: They embrace a novel experience outside their usual medium, going to "a ceramics workshop" and later adding "a texture I wouldn't have tried yesterday."
rationales.hedonism: The session emphasizes immediate enjoyment and sensory pleasure: "covered in clay, laughing at my lopsided bowl" and savoring the feeling of making things with a friend.

rationales.stimulation: He is drawn to uncertainty and challenge, fixating on "the feeling of standing at the edge of something that does not have a guaranteed outcome."
rationales.security: He explicitly pulls away from the safer plan to "Park the capital, let the reserves do their work" and instead keeps circling a risky new deal.

rationales.hedonism: The day is framed as restorative enjoyment: "Walked for hours, barely talked, ate pannenkoeken" and "It was exactly what I needed."
